message:
feat(server): implement tag-based filtering for tools, prompts, resources

Adds comprehensive tag filtering support to MCP list operations, enabling
clients to filter server components by metadata tags. This brings fastmcp-rust
to feature parity with the Python FastMCP's tag filtering capabilities.

## Core Implementation (router.rs)

New TagFilters struct:
- include: Option<&[String]> - components must have ALL tags (AND logic)
- exclude: Option<&[String]> - components rejected if they have ANY tag (OR)
- Case-insensitive tag matching throughout
- TagFilters::new() and TagFilters::matches() for construction and evaluation

Updated list methods with tag filtering:
- tools_filtered(session_state, tag_filters) -> Vec<Tool>
- prompts_filtered(session_state, tag_filters) -> Vec<Prompt>
- resources_filtered(session_state, tag_filters) -> Vec<Resource>

Filter logic:
- Empty include array = no filter (all components pass)
- Include: must contain ALL specified tags to be included
- Exclude: rejected if contains ANY specified tag
- Session state filtering still applies (disabled components excluded)

## Protocol Support (messages.rs)

Extended list request types with tag parameters:
- ListToolsParams: +include_tags, +exclude_tags
- ListPromptsParams: +include_tags, +exclude_tags
- ListResourcesParams: +include_tags, +exclude_tags

All fields are Option<Vec<String>> for backward compatibility.

## Client Updates (lib.rs)

Minor adjustments to support new message structures.

## Server Integration (lib.rs)

Wired up tag filters in handle_list_* methods to use new router APIs.

## Test Coverage (tests.rs)

Added ~200 lines of new tests covering:
- Tag include filtering (single and multiple tags, AND logic)
- Tag exclude filtering (single and multiple tags, OR logic)
- Combined include + exclude filtering
- Case-insensitive matching
- Empty filter arrays behavior
- Integration tests for tools, prompts, and resources

This enables clients like Claude Desktop to filter available tools
by category (e.g., "production", "experimental", "admin") without
server-side configuration changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
